evidence: agent/conncheck.c:929-1083 selects a highest-priority valid pair and schedules a separate triggered check
agent/conncheck.c:2900-2918 adds USE-CANDIDATE only according to the configured nomination mode
agent/conncheck.c:3701-3711 publishes the selected pair only after the pair is nominated
stun/usages/ice.c:273-300 compares tie-breakers and switches role or sends 487
agent/conncheck.c:3382-3412 recalculates pair priorities after a role change
agent/agent.c:1272-1274 generates the tie-breaker through the rejected default NiceRNG
agent/conncheck.c:1227-1263 implements expiry and failure timing
agent/agent.c:5781-5785 blocks application send after consent is lost
agent/conncheck.c:4348-4358 updates consent without checking response class, transaction identity, or source tuple
agent/conncheck.c:4942-4945 routes every otherwise-unmapped validated response to that consent updater
stun/stunagent.c:218-226 exempts 300, 400, 401, and 438 errors from credential validation
stun/stunagent.c:218-226 exempts TRY-ALTERNATE errors from credential validation
agent/conncheck.c:4027-4042 accepts ALTERNATE-SERVER after transaction-id matching
agent/conncheck.c:3931-3980 mutates TURN destinations and immediately creates a TCP or TLS TURN socket
agent/conncheck.c:3770-3811 similarly replaces the STUN discovery destination
stun/stunmessage.c:598-665 validates full message length, padding, attribute headers, and attribute bounds
stun/stunagent.c:161-169 rejects buffers whose parsed length differs from the received length
stun/stunagent.c:196-215 requires response transaction ids to match a saved request
agent/agent.c:2765-2769 releases resolver data before later list removal
agent/agent.c:2789-2819 dereferences the stream returned by agent_find_stream without a null check
agent/agent.c:4030-4045 removes a stream after cancelling TURN resolution but not the agent-wide STUN resolution
agent/component.c:343-351 documents a reliably triggerable pseudo-TCP graceful-close race
agent/debug.c:84-112 enables STUN diagnostics from NICE_DEBUG or G_MESSAGES_DEBUG
stun/stunagent.c:317-320 and 656-658 print HMAC keys and integrity values
agent/conncheck.c:1415-1425 and 2881-2897 print ICE usernames and passwords
agent/conncheck.c:4404-4434 and socket/socks5.c:115-124 print credentials
agent/agent.c:3695-3707 sends configured STUN strings to GResolver
agent/agent.c:3780-3794 schedules STUN resolution even when the configured string is numeric
agent/conncheck.c:3931-3980 changes destination and can create a socket without an application authorization callback
controlled profile requires immutable exact numeric tuple authorization before every library or socket boundary
nice/libnice.sym:1-132 exports agent, STUN, pseudo-TCP, and debug APIs rather than a narrow product ABI
agent/candidate.h:167-197 exposes credential pointers in NiceCandidate
agent/address.h:77-92 exposes a sockaddr-backed platform layout
random/random.c:54-68 provides a global RNG factory setter which is omitted from nice/libnice.sym
a product adapter would need opaque handles, copied ownership, stable errors, callback serialization, symbol hiding, and an audited RNG boundary

findings.title: ICE credentials and tie-breakers use the non-cryptographic GLib global PRNG
evidence: random/random.c:54-59 selects nice_rng_glib_new by default
random/random-glib.c:45-77 uses g_random_set_seed and g_random_int_range
agent/stream.c:124-130 generates ICE ufrag and password through NiceRNG
agent/agent.c:1272-1274 generates the 64-bit tie-breaker through the same NiceRNG
random/random.c:65-68 exposes only an internal global replacement hook omitted from nice/libnice.sym

findings.title: Reachable diagnostics emit ICE, TURN, SOCKS, and HMAC secret material
evidence: agent/debug.c:94-112 enables STUN logging from process environment flags
stun/stunagent.c:317-320 and 656-658 emit HMAC key bytes
agent/conncheck.c:1415-1425 and 2881-2897 emit ICE passwords
agent/agent.c:2381-2389 and 3288-3291 emit candidate and TURN passwords
socket/socks5.c:115-124 emits proxy credentials

findings.title: STUN and TURN alternate-server handling changes destinations before caller authorization
evidence: stun/stunagent.c:218-226 skips credential validation for TRY-ALTERNATE
agent/conncheck.c:3770-3811 applies a STUN alternate server
agent/conncheck.c:4027-4042 applies a TURN alternate server after transaction-id matching
agent/conncheck.c:3931-3980 mutates destinations and immediately creates a TCP or TLS TURN socket

findings.title: Consent freshness accepts otherwise-unmapped responses without success-class or source-tuple binding
evidence: agent/conncheck.c:4348-4358 unconditionally updates selected-pair consent time
agent/conncheck.c:4942-4945 calls the updater for every otherwise-unmapped validated response
the updater receives no source address and performs no transaction-id comparison
stun/stunagent.c:218-226 permits credential-free 300, 400, 401, and 438 error responses

findings.title: Stream removal does not safely bound an outstanding STUN resolver callback
evidence: agent/agent.c:2765-2769 frees resolver data before using its pointer for list removal
agent/agent.c:2789-2819 does not null-check a removed stream before dereference
agent/agent.c:4030-4045 cancels TURN resolution but not STUN resolution during stream removal

findings.title: Pseudo-TCP graceful shutdown contains a documented reliably triggerable race
evidence: agent/component.c:343-351 closes the underlying socket before the FIN handshake can complete

findings.title: The upstream ABI is too broad and platform-coupled for direct product exposure
evidence: nice/libnice.sym:1-132 exports STUN, pseudo-TCP, and debug controls
agent/candidate.h:167-197 exposes pointers and caller-visible ownership
agent/address.h:77-92 exposes sockaddr-backed storage
random/random.c:65-68 relies on a non-public global RNG replacement hook
